figures.caption: Fig. 1. Illustration of (a) existing DAOD task, (b) OSOD task and (c) AOOD task. Foggy weather corresponds to the target domain, whereas clear weather corresponds to the source domain.
TABLE VIII SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS OF THE HYPERPARAMETER K ON CITYSCAPES → FOGGY CITYSCAPES UNDER HET-SEM SETTING (5 NOVEL CLASSES). THE BEST RESULTS ARE HIGHLIGHTED IN BOLD.
TABLE III COMPARING WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART VALIDATION RESULTS ON PASCAL VOC → CLIPART.(NUM. INDICATES THE NUMBER OF NOVEL CLASSES.) THE TOP 2 RESULTS ARE SHOWN IN RED, GREEN.
Fig. 6. The boxplot for Pascal VOC → CLipart, where the blue dots • and the red stars ⋆ indicate the performance of the baseline and CCKM, respectively.
TABLE I COMPARING WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART VALIDATION RESULTS ON CITYSCAPES → FOGGY CITYSCAPES. THE TOP 2 RESULTS ARE SHOWN IN RED, GREEN.

Fig. 9. Visualization of detection results in Cityscapes [36] → Foggy Cityscapes [37] dataset. The first column shows the groundtruth; the second and third columns visualize the detection results of SOMA [22] and CCKM, respectively. In the last column, the closeups of false positive (FP) and false negative (FN) errors are zoomed in red and yellow boxes. The detection errors of SOMA are described in the captions below each row.
TABLE II COMPARING WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART VALIDATION RESULTS ON CITYSCAPES → BDD100K. THE TOP 2 RESULTS ARE SHOWN IN RED, GREEN.
